verses.text: They bring a palsied man; Jesus says, 'Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.' Deeper need met. He heals the soul before the body.
They bring a palsied man; Jesus says, 'Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.' Deeper need met. He heals the soul before the body.
The scribes charge blasphemy; Jesus asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal? A telling question. The power to heal proves the power to forgive.
The scribes charge blasphemy; Jesus asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal? A telling question. The power to heal proves the power to forgive.
The scribes charge blasphemy; Jesus asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal? A telling question. The power to heal proves the power to forgive.
'That ye may know the Son of man hath power... to forgive sins,' He says, Arise and walk; the man does, and the crowds marvel. Authority shown. The visible cure confirms the unseen pardon.
'That ye may know the Son of man hath power... to forgive sins,' He says, Arise and walk; the man does, and the crowds marvel. Authority shown. The visible cure confirms the unseen pardon.
'That ye may know the Son of man hath power... to forgive sins,' He says, Arise and walk; the man does, and the crowds marvel. Authority shown. The visible cure confirms the unseen pardon.
He sees Matthew at the tax-booth, says, 'Follow me'; and he rises and follows. Instant obedience. The publican leaves his gain at a word.
Eating with publicans and sinners, Jesus says, 'I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.' The Physician. He came for the sick who own their sickness.
Eating with publicans and sinners, Jesus says, 'I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.' The Physician. He came for the sick who own their sickness.
Eating with publicans and sinners, Jesus says, 'I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.' The Physician. He came for the sick who own their sickness.
Eating with publicans and sinners, Jesus says, 'I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.' The Physician. He came for the sick who own their sickness.
Asked why His disciples fast not, He answers, the bridechamber children cannot mourn while the bridegroom is with them. A time for joy. Christ's presence is cause for gladness.
Asked why His disciples fast not, He answers, the bridechamber children cannot mourn while the bridegroom is with them. A time for joy. Christ's presence is cause for gladness.
No man sews new cloth on old, nor new wine in old bottles. Newness. The gospel is no patch on the old; it makes all things new.
No man sews new cloth on old, nor new wine in old bottles. Newness. The gospel is no patch on the old; it makes all things new.
A ruler pleads, My daughter is dead, but lay thy hand on her; Jesus follows. Faith in death. He believes Christ can raise even the dead.
A ruler pleads, My daughter is dead, but lay thy hand on her; Jesus follows. Faith in death. He believes Christ can raise even the dead.
A woman touches His hem; Jesus says, 'Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole.' Healing by faith. The trembling touch of faith draws virtue from Christ.
A woman touches His hem; Jesus says, 'Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole.' Healing by faith. The trembling touch of faith draws virtue from Christ.
A woman touches His hem; Jesus says, 'Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole.' Healing by faith. The trembling touch of faith draws virtue from Christ.
He puts out the mourners, takes the dead maid by the hand, and she arises. Death undone. At His touch the grave gives up its claim.
He puts out the mourners, takes the dead maid by the hand, and she arises. Death undone. At His touch the grave gives up its claim.
He puts out the mourners, takes the dead maid by the hand, and she arises. Death undone. At His touch the grave gives up its claim.
He puts out the mourners, takes the dead maid by the hand, and she arises. Death undone. At His touch the grave gives up its claim.
Two blind men cry, 'Son of David, have mercy'; 'according to your faith be it unto you,' and they see. Sight given. Faith opens the eyes that grace makes whole.
Two blind men cry, 'Son of David, have mercy'; 'according to your faith be it unto you,' and they see. Sight given. Faith opens the eyes that grace makes whole.

Two blind men cry, 'Son of David, have mercy'; 'according to your faith be it unto you,' and they see. Sight given. Faith opens the eyes that grace makes whole.
A dumb demoniac is healed; the Pharisees sneer that He casts out devils by the devil. Blind malice. They credit even His mercy to the powers of darkness.
A dumb demoniac is healed; the Pharisees sneer that He casts out devils by the devil. Blind malice. They credit even His mercy to the powers of darkness.
A dumb demoniac is healed; the Pharisees sneer that He casts out devils by the devil. Blind malice. They credit even His mercy to the powers of darkness.
Moved with compassion, He sees the harvest great and the labourers few; 'pray ye... the Lord of the harvest.' A field to reap. Christ's pity for the crowds calls for praying, sending hearts.
Moved with compassion, He sees the harvest great and the labourers few; 'pray ye... the Lord of the harvest.' A field to reap. Christ's pity for the crowds calls for praying, sending hearts.
